title: Agency Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities

source.name: New York Fed Markets Data Hub

summary: The Federal Reserve's Open Market Trading Desk at the New York Fed conducted agency CMBS purchases on behalf of SOMA from March 23, 2020 through November 4, 2021, purchasing securities backed by multifamily mortgages guaranteed by Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and Ginnie Mae. BlackRock Financial Markets Advisory was retained as a third-party vendor to operationalize these purchases but the contract was terminated on May 2, 2022.
key_points: FOMC directed the Desk to purchase agency CMBS effective March 23, 2020 as part of emergency pandemic response
Purchases were limited to agency CMBS secured primarily by multifamily home mortgages guaranteed by Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and Ginnie Mae
Desk ceased purchasing agency CMBS effective November 4, 2021 to sustain smooth functioning
BlackRock Financial Markets Advisory was retained on March 24, 2020 as investment manager; contract terminated May 2, 2022
Transaction data is published quarterly with approximately two-year lag per Dodd-Frank Act requirements
why_it_matters: This source documents a specific Federal Reserve balance sheet operation conducted during the COVID-19 crisis. The agency CMBS purchase program represents an unusual expansion of the Fed's securities holdings into commercial mortgage-backed securities, typically outside the traditional Treasury/agency MBS framework. Understanding the scope and timeline of these operations provides context for how the Fed deployed its balance sheet as a market support tool.
macro_context: The Fed's agency CMBS purchase program (March 2020 – November 2021) was a notable expansion of the Fed's balance sheet toolkit during the COVID-19 pandemic, extending purchases beyond traditional Treasury and agency MBS into commercial mortgage-backed securities. This operation was temporary and specifically designed for crisis response, with purchases ceasing when market functioning stabilized.
market_structure_context: The source describes the operational mechanics of how the Fed implemented agency CMBS purchases, including the use of an external investment manager (BlackRock) to transact with approved counterparties in the secondary market. This illustrates the Fed's operational framework for deploying balance sheet capacity through market intermediaries rather than direct purchases.
